23.reason: Word extraction by whitespace fails to capture words separated by punctuation without spaces; should use character-by-character reading.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Word extraction by whitespace fails to capture words separated by punctuation without spaces; should use character-by-character reading.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: fscanf reads tokens separated by whitespace only, but words may be separated by punctuation (e.g., "hello,world"). Should read line by line with fgets() and parse characters to correctly extract all words.]

25.reason: Using strlen(s) in loop condition is unsafe because modifying s inside changes its length.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Using strlen(s) in loop condition is unsafe because modifying s inside changes its length.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: The loop condition uses strlen(s) which changes as s is modified, causing early exit and missing characters after the first non-letter. Also, replacing non‑letters with '\0' truncates the string, losing all subsequent letters in the token. Proper word extraction requires iterating over characters and building words individually.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: strlen(s) is recalculated on every iteration. Modifying s[ou] to '\0' changes the string length dynamically, causing the loop to terminate prematurely and skip processing remaining characters.

30.reason: [Setting non-alphabetic characters to '\0' truncates the string prematurely, causing loss of subsequent alphabetic characters in the same token read by fscanf]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Setting non‑letter to '\0' truncates the string at the first non‑letter, losing the rest of the token.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: Setting a non‑letter to '\0' truncates the string at this position. For example, "hello123world" becomes "\0" after the first non‑letter? Actually, it becomes "hello\0", and "world" is lost. Moreover, the loop may exit prematurely because strlen decreases. Should instead split the token into separate words.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Setting non-alphabetic characters to '\0' truncates the string prematurely, causing loss of subsequent alphabetic characters in the same token read by fscanf]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This truncates the string at the first non-letter character, but leaves the rest of the string intact. Subsequent letters after a non-letter are not processed as separate words, violating the problem's word definition.]

20.reason: Declaration after statement violates C89/C90 standard, which causes a compilation error on strict compilers.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Compilation error in C89/C90: variable declarations must appear before any executable statements within a block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Declaration after statement violates C89/C90 standard, which causes a compilation error on strict compilers.
